business.headline: FDs, SCSS or mutual funds? The best way to invest Rs 50 lakh after retirementRetiring with Rs 50 lakh may sound reassuring, but the real challenge begins after retirement: how do you turn that lump sum into a steady monthly income without running out of money?

US Justice Department okays Paramount's $110 billion Warner Bros takeoverAfter an eight-month review, the DOJ concluded the proposed merger would strengthen competition across streaming, television and film, potentially benefiting consumers, workers and the broader entertainment industry.

SpaceX opens at $150 in strong Nasdaq debut, up 11% from IPO priceSpaceX surged more than 20 per cent in its Nasdaq debut, lifting its valuation above USD 2 trillion as investors piled into the world's biggest IPO. The stock opened at USD 150, signalling strong demand and easing concerns over its market debut.

Groceries, fuel, school fees: Is your monthly budget about to get tighter?School fees, petrol, vegetables, utility bills—the list of monthly expenses never seems to end. As inflation rises, many families are asking the same question: is their budget about to get even tighter?

Inflation rises to 3.93% for May as West Asia conflict pushes up fuel and food pricesFood inflation continued to move higher in May. It stood at 4.78%, up from 4.20% in April, showing that prices of food items are rising faster than they were a month earlier.

Markets rally on easing West Asia tensions, oil falling below $88The S&P BSE Sensex surged 1,695.40 points, or 2.30%, to close at 75,527.95, while the NSE Nifty50 jumped 461.30 points, or 1.99%, to settle at 23,622.90.

Salary mismatch to missed interest: 7 ITR mistakes that can trigger tax noticesFiling your ITR may seem straightforward, but even a minor reporting error can attract the attention of the tax department. Here are seven mistakes you should watch out for.

The world's biggest IPO starts trading today. Is Elon Musk's SpaceX overvalued?SpaceX begins trading on Nasdaq after a record $75 billion IPO and a $1.75 trillion valuation. The debut has sharpened questions over valuation, Musk's control, xAI losses and broader market liquidity.

FD rates up to 8.10%: Check these small finance banks offering the highest returnsIn a world of rising uncertainty, guaranteed returns are becoming more valuable. Here's a look at the small finance banks offering FD rates as high as 8.10% this month.

Govt's new petrol, diesel order: Who is affected, and why was it introduced?The Centre has restricted industrial and commercial bulk fuel purchases from retail pumps. The move aims to protect pump supplies for ordinary consumers amid a wide retail-bulk price gap.

Earning Rs 20 lakh? Claim these tax benefits under the new tax regimeThink the new tax regime leaves no room for tax savings? If you earn Rs 20 lakh a year, you may be surprised to learn that several tax-friendly benefits are still available.
Gold demand picks up despite PM's appeal to avoid buyingAt the time of writing at 11.45 am, MCX gold was trading at Rs 1,49,942, up Rs 1,021, while silver stood at Rs 2,42,232, higher by Rs 2,579.

India may let fiscal deficit widen as US-Iran war raises oil costsIndia may allow its fiscal deficit to widen to 4.8% of GDP as the Iran war raises oil-linked costs. The move reflects pressure from higher subsidies, costlier imports and possible spending cuts.

Inflation fears return: Will Indians have to spend more on everyday items?Rising global oil prices and stronger US inflation are reviving concerns over India's household costs. Economists and the RBI say risks are building even without an immediate inflation shock.

Sensex jumped 1,700 points: 3 reasons why stock market rose todayThe S&P BSE Sensex surged 1,695.40 points, or 2.30%, to close at 75,527.95, while the NSE Nifty50 jumped 461.30 points, or 1.99%, to settle at 23,622.90.

Markets surge as crude oil falls below $90 amid hopes of US-Iran breakthroughThe S&P BSE Sensex surged 873.88 points, or 1.18%, to 74,706.43 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 climbed 236.40 points, or 1.02%, to 23,398.00.

World's first trillionaire Elon Musk is now richer than Taiwan after SpaceX IPOElon Musk became the world's first trillionaire after SpaceX's record IPO lifted his holdings. The milestone pushed his fortune past Taiwan's economy and widened the gap with every other billionaire.

Centre restricts bulk industrial fuel purchases at petrol pumpsThe government has barred industrial, commercial and institutional users from buying petrol and diesel at retail pumps. The move seeks to prevent diversion, curb shortages and protect supplies for ordinary vehicle owners.

US drug agency puts Dabur products on import alert after inspection flags lapsesThe US FDA has placed drugs manufactured at Dabur India's Dadra and Nagar Haveli factory under an import alert after an inspection found data integrity and maintenance lapses.

Steady adjustment of rupee is okay, a free fall is not: Raghuram RajanIn an exclusive interview with India Today TV, former Reserve Bank of India Governor Raghuram Rajan shares his thoughts on how to deal with the ongoing conflict in West Asia's impact on the global and Indian economies and more.

entertainment.headline: Sushant Singh Rajput death anniversary: Sister Shweta shares a moving note Sushant Singh Rajput's sister, Shweta Singh Kirti, remembered the late actor ahead of his sixth death anniversary with an emotional Instagram note. She also shared an AI-generated image of him as Lord Ram.

Karan Aujla, Virat Kohli's crossover leaves fans counting down to June 21 Virat Kohli and Karan Aujla are set to come together for a One8 event in Delhi on June 21. The announcement has already generated massive buzz online as fans are eager to see the cricket icon and Punjabi hitmaker share the spotlight.

Shaved head and leather jacket: Salman Khan grabs attention at Aamir Khan's event Salman Khan attended the celebrations marking 25 years of Aamir Khan Productions on Saturday. The actor's latest look drew attention as he posed with Aamir Khan and Kareena Kapoor at the star-studded event.

Kangana Ranaut says she has nothing against Diljit Dosanjh: We've never met Actor-politician Kangana Ranaut said her clash with actor-singer Diljit Dosanjh was only over differing views and not personal. Her remarks revisit their 2020 farmers' protests row as their respective new films open on the same day.

Himanshu Jangra apologises again, says viral biryani story wasn't totally true Himanshu Jangra has opened up about the fallout from the controversy linked to Pranit More's show. In a conversation with his former boss, he spoke about the trolling, his firing, his family's reaction and why he now regrets attending the event.

Ravi Kishan's old Dhurandhar gets a surprise second innings, actor reacts Actor Ravi Kishan's 2013 Bhojpuri action film Dhurandhar: The Shooter is seeing a surprise revival online. The actor recently reacted to the surge in the film's popularity and joked that the film's producer must have become a millionaire.

Theatres are packed with releases, but are audiences turning up? Trade experts decode A crowded June 12 release slate has set up a direct test of audience appetite across genres and languages. Trade experts say the real outcome will depend on word of mouth, screen access and weekend walk-ins.

How the Ranga-Billa case, which inspired Raakh, impacted Bobby Deol's childhood As Raakh revisits the 1978 Ranga-Billa case, Bobby Deol recalled that his childhood friend was kidnapped by the duo and escaped. He reflected on how the crime left a lasting mark on families and children beyond the headlines.

Prioritise our films: Anurag Kashyap on Bandar's struggle against Obsession Filmmaker Anurag Kashyap criticised theatres for giving limited shows to Indian films like Bandar while the Hollywood film Obsession continued to dominate screens. He questioned how local cinema could grow without better screen allocation and visibility.

Kunickaa Sadanand backs 'good boy' Pranit More, hopes people will forgive him After Pranit More issued a fresh apology in the Rs 370 biryani row, Kunickaa Sadanand urged people to forgive him. She praised the comedian for taking responsibility and owning up to his mistake.

Manoj Bajpayee reveals Yash Chopra paid him lead actor's fee for cameo in Veer-Zaara Manoj Bajpayee recalled Yash Chopra asking him to do a cameo in Veer-Zaara after watching Pinjar. He revealed that the late filmmaker paid him the same fee he charged for a lead role in a film.

Mouni Roy shuts down trolls, addresses rumours about her sexual orientation Mouni Roy addressed rumours about her sexual orientation during a YouTube chat after her separation from Suraj Nambiar. She said her female friends and family supported her through the difficult period.

Ram Charan's Peddi earns Rs 366 crore, beats South India's 2026 box office records Ram Charan's Peddi has crossed Rs 366 crore worldwide in its nine-day run, according to official figures. The film has become South India's highest-grossing release of 2026 so far.

BTS at 13: What seven Korean men and millions of ARMYs taught me about life BTS celebrate their 13th debut anniversary on June 13, 2026. They will be performing in Busan. Tracing the life lessons learnt from BTS and their 13-year journey. Their story resonates deeply with fans, inspiring millions across India to embrace vulnerability and self-love while chasing dreams.

Pranit More issues fresh apology over Rs 370 biryani row Stand-up comedian Pranit More has issued a public apology following massive social media outrage over a viral video featuring a misogynistic remark made by an audience member.

Main Vaapas Aaunga box office day 1: Imtiaz Ali film trails behind Love Aaj Kal 2 Imtiaz Ali's Main Vaapas Aaunga grossed Rs 1.38 crore on its opening day. The modest start left it well below Love Aaj Kal 2 as the film heads into the weekend.

I deserve hate: Pranit More issues fresh apology over Rs 370 biryani row Pranit More has issued a fresh apology over Rs 370 biryani row after backlash over his longer viral video from his controversial event.

David Beckham gets Walk of Fame star, Tom Cruise calls his journey a Hollywood story Tom Cruise praised David Beckham as he received his star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame. He compared Beckham's journey, family and legacy to a true Hollywood story.

25 years later, I watched Lagaan for the first time. It still hits for six Twenty-five years later, I watched Lagaan and it still feels astonishingly alive. What could have easily become a relic of another era remains a deeply moving story about hope, community, nation-building and standing up against impossible odds.

general news.headline: Blockade violation won't be tolerated: Rubio to Jaishankar as India protests attacksThe conversation between Marco Rubio and S Jaishankar occurred days after a US attack killed three Indian seafarers off the Oman Coast. Jaishankar reiterated India's strong protest over the attack during the conversation with Marco Rubio.

US-Iran peace deal to be signed on June 14, Hormuz will open immediately: TrumpUS President Donald Trump also said the deal would ensure Iran does not acquire nuclear weapons, describing it as placing a "wall to no nuclear weapon." He contrasted the deal with past US policy, specifically the Obama administration's alleged financial arrangements with Tehran.

FIFA World Cup Live: Ancelotti's men disappoint in 1-1 drawBrazil (BRA) vs Morocco (MAR), FIFA World Cup 2026 Live score: Brazil's World Cup campaign begins with frustration as Morocco earn a deserved draw. Carlo Ancelotti's side struggled for cohesion, while the African champions once again impressed.

PM, Trump to meet on G7 Summit sidelines on June 17; trade deal on agendaThe meeting will mark the first face-to-face interaction between the two leaders since February 2025, when Prime Minister Modi visited President Trump following his victory in the November 2024 US presidential election.

Sushant Singh Rajput death anniversary: Sister Shweta shares a moving noteSushant Singh Rajput's sister, Shweta Singh Kirti, remembered the late actor ahead of his sixth death anniversary with an emotional Instagram note. She also shared an AI-generated image of him as Lord Ram.

Brazil's ethanol lesson for India: A Rs 20 difference may not be enoughWith the launch of Delhi's inaugural ethanol fuel pump, India took its first step into the E85 era. However, Brazil's experience shows that lower pump rates alone may not guarantee widespread consumer adoption.

PM Modi lands in France on first leg of two-nation visit, to hold talks with MacronIn Nice, PM Modi was received by a delegation of senior French dignitaries, including Minister of Education Edouard Geffray, Minister of Higher Education, Research and Space Philippe Baptiste and France's Ambassador to India, Thierry Mathou.

Qatar steal historic first World Cup point as Switzerland pay for missed chancesQatar secured the first World Cup point in their history as captain Boualem Khoukhi's stoppage-time header earned a dramatic 1-1 draw against Switzerland, who paid for a host of missed chances.

Why US govt banned all foreigners from using Anthropic Claude Fable 5 & MythosThe US has forced Anthropic to block access to its most advanced AI models for all foreign nationals, including Indians. But why is Washington getting possessive about cutting-edge AI, and what does it mean for countries like India that still rely heavily on foreign technology? Lets take a closer look.

How Gautam Gambhir became the architect of 2nd fastest ODI hundred vs IndiaRahmanullah Gurbaz's 48-ball hundred lit up the Dharamsala skies on Saturday. Playing the opening ODI of the 3-match series, Gurbaz entertained the crowd with one of the most destructive ODI knocks played against India.

Brazil vs Morocco spoilt by streaming glitches for World Cup fans in IndiaFIFA World Cup 2026: Brazil's World Cup opener against Morocco should have been a marquee occasion for Indian football fans. Instead, many viewers once again reported crashes, buffering and login issues, reviving frustrations that first surfaced during the tournament opener.

Cashier shot dead by masked gunmen at Chandigarh shop, police suspect old rivalryGunmen opened fire on cashier Jaanki Dass inside a medical store in Chandigarh's Sector 11 on Saturday afternoon. Police have imposed nakabandi, checked CCTV footage and are probing whether old threats or another motive led to the attack.

No India at the FIFA World Cup, but 4 Indian-origin stars are ready to shineAlthough India will not be at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, four players with Indian roots will be part of the tournament. New Zealand's Sarpreet Singh, Qatar's Tahsin Mohammed Jamshid, DR Congo's Samuel Moutoussamy and Australia's Nishan Velupillay will all represent their respective countries.

Russian tourist found dead under mysterious circumstances in Kasol homestayA 29-year-old Russian tourist was found dead at a homestay in Kasol after her friend discovered her unconscious in the washroom. Police have informed the Russian Embassy and are awaiting the post-mortem report as they examine all possible angles.

No subscription needed: Here's how you can watch World Cup for free in IndiaThe FIFA World Cup 2026 is all set to begin, with the fans in India eagerly waiting to watch each game. Here's how you can watch the matches for free in India.

Iran booby-traps uranium sites with mines as deal with US takes shape: ReportIran has fortified tunnels holding highly enriched uranium as it moves closer to a possible deal with the US. The move could complicate verification, recovery and removal of the stockpile even if an agreement is sealed.

Kangana Ranaut says she has nothing against Diljit Dosanjh: We've never metActor-politician Kangana Ranaut said her clash with actor-singer Diljit Dosanjh was only over differing views and not personal. Her remarks revisit their 2020 farmers' protests row as their respective new films open on the same day.
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

TMC turmoil deepens as Sudip Bandyopadhyay joins rebels after meeting with ShahTMC announced a wide reshuffle after senior leaders were reportedly seen at Bhupendra Yadav's Delhi residence. The timing has intensified talk of internal churn, though the party has not linked the moves to that meeting.

Video: Customs find gold worth Rs 4.27 crore inside IndiGo flight's speaker boxAccording to Customs officials, the manner of concealment indicated that the gold had been hidden by an unidentified person with the intention of smuggling it into India in violation of the Customs Act, 1962.

Prioritise our films: Anurag Kashyap on Bandar's struggle against ObsessionFilmmaker Anurag Kashyap criticised theatres for giving limited shows to Indian films like Bandar while the Hollywood film Obsession continued to dominate screens. He questioned how local cinema could grow without better screen allocation and visibility.

Mystery woman caught on CCTV before deadly Delhi blaze, police suspect foul playPolice are tracing a woman seen on CCTV entering a Govindpuri building moments before a fatal fire. Her presence has intensified suspicion that the blaze may have been deliberately triggered.

ED Party ka damaad: Kejriwal targets BJP over Lawrence Bishnoi, Gujarat drug routeArvind Kejriwal accused the Centre in Jalandhar of protecting Lawrence Bishnoi in Sabarmati jail. He also linked the row to Punjab's anti-drug campaign and alleged Gujarat had become a narcotics gateway.

Couple says DNA test showed IVF twins not biologically theirs, demands answersIn an exclusive interview with India Today, the couple said that they went through the IVF treatment at a reputed hospital. However, when the babies were born, they bore no resemblance to them.

sports.headline: Vinicius Jr's World Cup stunner sends Travis Scott wild in Brazil vs Morocco clash Vinicius Junior produced a stunning equaliser against Morocco in Brazil's FIFA World Cup 2026 opener, with the spectacular strike leaving fans in awe and American rapper Travis Scott celebrating wildly in the stands.

Even my building watchman says, lose to anyone but not Pakistan: Jemimah Jemimah Rodrigues said India cannot ignore the pressure of facing Pakistan at the Women's T20 World Cup. Her remarks underlined the rivalry's weight as India prepare for high-pressure moments through scenario-based training.

Brazil vs Morocco spoilt by streaming glitches for World Cup fans in India FIFA World Cup 2026: Brazil's World Cup opener against Morocco should have been a marquee occasion for Indian football fans. Instead, many viewers once again reported crashes, buffering and login issues, reviving frustrations that first surfaced during the tournament opener.

Was Rohit Sharma angry? Shubman Gill addresses run-out drama in Dharamshala Shubman Gill said Rohit Sharma was fine after their run-out mix-up against Afghanistan. The repeat of Mohali's chaos ended without an outburst, even as Gill's 84 and slips catch drove India's win.

Qatar steal historic first World Cup point as Switzerland pay for missed chances Qatar secured the first World Cup point in their history as captain Boualem Khoukhi's stoppage-time header earned a dramatic 1-1 draw against Switzerland, who paid for a host of missed chances.

West Indies' Campbelle survives and sinks defending champions New Zealand Shemaine Campbelle's unbeaten 88 helped West Indies chase down 163 in a last-over thriller, handing defending champions New Zealand an early setback after a costly display of dropped catches and missed chances.

Nitish Reddy pauses press conference to take mother's call after India win Nitish Kumar Reddy paused his post-match press conference in Dharamsala to take a call from his mother after India's win over Afghanistan. The brief exchange became a talking point online, with fans praising the all-rounder for staying grounded.

Gurnoor steams, Gill flows as India sweep Afghanistan in rain-hit Dharamsala ODI India maintained their dominance over Afghanistan in the ongoing bilateral series, thrashing them by seven wickets in the first ODI of the three-match contest. Gujarat Titans stars Gurnoor Brar and Shubman Gill led India's all-round performance against the visitors.

No handshake with Pakistan? Harmanpreet Kaur breaks silence ahead of big clash Harmanpreet Kaur declined to say whether India will shake hands with Pakistan before Sunday's Women's T20 World Cup clash. Her answer kept the spotlight on cricket, even as the fixture again carried the pressure and politics that surround this rivalry.

FIFA World Cup Live: (BRA 1-1 MAR) Ancelotti makes final roll of the dice Brazil (BRA) vs Morocco (MAR), FIFA World Cup 2026 Live score: Brazil make one final change as Bruno Guimaraes departs. Danilo Santos is handed the task of helping the Selecao find a late breakthrough in a match that remains finely poised.

There is pressure for Pakistan clash, but we want to enjoy the game: Harmanpreet KaurWomen's T20 World Cup 2026, IND vs PAK: Harmanpreet Kaur acknowledged the pressure of the India vs Pakistan clash but said the team will focus on enjoying the occasion and playing their best cricket, treating it like any other match.

Amid Neeraj's injury crisis, 2 javelin stars qualify for Commonwealth GamesJavelin powerhouse India were at a real risk of missing out on the Commonwealth Games with Neeraj Chopra continuing to recover from his back injury. However, on June 13, two other Indian stars cleared the CWG qualification mark in the final event before selections were set to be finalised.

How Gautam Gambhir became the architect of 2nd fastest ODI hundred vs IndiaRahmanullah Gurbaz's 48-ball hundred lit up the Dharamsala skies on Saturday. Playing the opening ODI of the 3-match series, Gurbaz entertained the crowd with one of the most destructive ODI knocks played against India.

Shubman Gill stuns Dharamsala with flying slip catch against AfghanistanIND vs AFG, 1st ODI: Shubman Gill produced a stunning one-handed catch in the slips to dismiss Afghanistan's Allah Ghazanfar during the first ODI in Dharamsala, leaving fans in awe.

Pakistan skipper injured? Fatima Sana shares update before blockbuster India clashPakistan captain Fatima Sana said she was fine after taking a blow to the knee in training. The update came as Pakistan prepared for a tough group after two warm-up defeats.

Gurbaz lights up Dharamsala with fastest ODI hundred by Afghanistan batterIndia vs Afghanistan: Rahmanullah Gurbaz lit up the skies of Dharamsala with a lightning-quick hundred. The batter registered the fastest ODI hundred by an Afghan batter in the rain-affected ODI of the 3-match series.

AUS vs SA Highlights: Australia hammer South Africa by 65 runs in ManchesterWomens T20 World Cup, AUS vs SA Highlights: Australia crushed South Africa by 65 runs in the Group A clash at the Old Trafford to begin their campaign in a stupendous manner.

Fresh off dream India debut, Manav Suthar makes instant impact in county cricketManav Suthar carried his India form into county cricket with 3 for 85 on his Warwickshire debut against Yorkshire. Suthar had an incredible start to his Test career with seven wickets against Afghanistan in the one-off Test.

PV Sindhu, Lakshya Sen to lead India's badminton squads for Asian GamesPV Sindhu and Lakshya Sen will spearhead India's badminton squads for the 2026 Asian Games in Aichi-Nagoya. The selection underlines India's blend of proven medal winners and rising international performers.

Kathryn Bryce inspires Scotland to historic first-ever win in Women's T20 World CupWomen's World Cup, SCO vs IRE: Kathryn Bryce scored 60 runs and picked up two wickets as Scotland beat Ireland by 40 runs on Saturday at the Old Trafford in Manchester.

Rohit Sharma becomes India's oldest male ODI cricketerIND vs AFG, 1st ODI: Rohit Sharma became the oldest male cricketer to play an ODI for India, breaking a 37-year-old record held by Mohinder Amarnath during the first ODI against Afghanistan in Dharamsala.

Missed one-off Test, but Gurnoor Brar and Harsh Dubey make ODI debuts in DharamsalaGurnoor Brar and Harsh Dubey received their maiden India ODI caps in the series opener against Afghanistan at Dharamsala. Rain reduced the match to 25 overs a side as Shubman Gill chose to bowl in Rohit Sharma's return.

India will use ODI glory as fuel for Women's T20 World Cup: Amol MuzumdarAmol Muzumdar said India will draw confidence from their ODI World Cup triumph as they begin their Women's T20 World Cup campaign, while stressing that T20 cricket is a completely different challenge.

BCCI evaluating feasibility of bilaterals, IPL likely from early MarchThe Indian Cricket Board is assessing the feasibility of bilateral cricket while considering a larger window for the Indian Premier League. A BCCI source suggested that work is already underway as the tournament looks to expand to a 94-game format.

New Zealand's Jess Kerr refuses to let facial paralysis, diabetes crush her dreamsWomen's T20 World Cup: Despite battling Bell's Palsy, Type 1 diabetes and career-ending running injuries, Jess Kerr refused to let adversity define her, forging a successful international cricket career with New Zealand.

He had 3 countries. Folarin Balogun chose US and scored twice on World Cup debutFolarin Balogun was born in Brooklyn, raised in London, and eligible for Nigeria. He chose the United States three years ago. On Friday night, he scored twice on his World Cup debut to justify every bit of that faith.

No India at the FIFA World Cup, but 4 Indian-origin stars are ready to shineAlthough India will not be at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, four players with Indian roots will be part of the tournament. New Zealand's Sarpreet Singh, Qatar's Tahsin Mohammed Jamshid, DR Congo's Samuel Moutoussamy and Australia's Nishan Velupillay will all represent their respective countries.

Danni Wyatt Hodge celebrates century with touching cradle gesture for daughter: WatchDanni Wyatt Hodge celebrated both her fifty and unbeaten century against Sri Lanka with a touching cradle gesture dedicated to her newborn daughter Daisy, creating one of the most emotional moments of the Women's T20 World Cup 2026.

Can Carlo Ancelotti end Brazil's 24-year wait or is World Cup a different game?Carlo Ancelotti has conquered club football for more than two decades, but the Brazil job presents a different challenge altogether. As the Selecao begin their World Cup campaign, the Italian is chasing both a sixth star and a place in Brazilian football folklore.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
